response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned polyglot engineer with strong TypeScript and distributed-systems credentials, and his current role touches MCP server development and agentic workflows. However, the role requires 8+ years building AI-driven solutions—his AI exposure appears to begin only in April 2025. Python, the primary language listed in the JD, is absent from his skills. There is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI, which are core to the role. These are not peripheral gaps; they are the foundation of the position.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse, staff-level engineering experience across web, embedded, mobile, and distributed systems, demonstrating broad technical adaptability
Direct, hands-on work with Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure in his current role, which is directly relevant to the team's tooling
Proven data pipeline experience at scale (millions of real-time HTTP requests at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) and cloud infrastructure work (AWS CDK at Optilyz)
concerns: AI-specific experience is limited to roughly one month (April 2025 onward); the role demands 8+ years of production AI delivery, a gap of nearly a decade
Python is not listed anywhere in skills or work history, yet it is the primary language called out in both required qualifications and key technologies
No evidence of vector databases, RAG architecture, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks—the technical core of the forward-deployed GenAI scope
